---
name: Imported project source
description: When a Replit zip import only contains git metadata, clone the real source from the linked GitHub remote.
---

# Imported project source

## Rule
When a user imports a project via zip and the archive only contains `.git/`, `.replit`, `.env`, and an expired download token, the actual source code is almost always in the GitHub repository named in the git remote config. Do not assume the project is empty; extract the remote URL and clone the source.

**Why:** The zip upload may be a shallow wrapper generated by an export tool that failed to fetch the full repository contents.

**How to apply:**
1. Read `.git/config` or `git remote -v` for the origin URL.
2. Use the stored token (Replit Secret or `.env` value) if needed to clone.
3. Copy the source files into the workspace root, preserving the project's own structure.
